properties.text: The fork trigger MUST only fire at epoch boundaries, i.e., when state.slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0. For all slots where slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH != 0, the fork trigger MUST NOT initiate an upgrade.

reachability.notes: Fork trigger is activated by slot progression which is driven by network time and P2P consensus. An attacker cannot directly control it, but misconfigured epoch constants could cause issues reachable via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Violation would cause fork to trigger at non-epoch boundary, causing consensus split across the network as nodes disagree on when to upgrade.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating fork trigger at non-epoch-boundary slot causing consensus divergence.

properties.text: The fork trigger MUST fire exactly once at FULU_FORK_EPOCH. For all epochs e != FULU_FORK_EPOCH, the fork trigger MUST NOT initiate an upgrade. After the fork has triggered, no subsequent call to the fork trigger may re-trigger the upgrade.

reachability.notes: Fork epoch is a hardcoded constant; incorrect configuration causes network-wide failure.

properties.severity_justification: Triggering fork at wrong epoch or multiple times would cause permanent state corruption and consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing fork trigger at incorrect epoch or double-trigger.

properties.text: The fork upgrade is irreversible: once upgrade_to_fulu(state) has been applied, the resulting post-state MUST have fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION, and no state transition may revert the fork version to a pre-Fulu version.

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

properties.severity_justification: Fork oscillation would cause network instability and potential consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing fork reversion or oscillation.

properties.text: For all fields F in pre-state (electra.BeaconState) except fork and proposer_lookahead: post.F == pre.F. The upgrade_to_fulu function MUST preserve all state fields except the explicitly modified ones.

reachability.notes: State preservation during fork is consensus-critical. Triggered automatically at fork epoch via P2P consensus.

properties.severity_justification: Dropping or corrupting any state field during fork would cause immediate consensus failure and potential fund loss (e.g., corrupted balances).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating state field corruption during upgrade_to_fulu.

properties.text: After upgrade_to_fulu: post.fork.previous_version == pre.fork.current_version. The fork version history chain MUST be maintained.

reachability.notes: Incorrect version chaining would cause P2P fork digest mismatches.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect previous_version breaks fork digest computation, causing P2P network partition.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing fork version chain breakage.

properties.text: After upgrade_to_fulu: post.fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION. The post-state MUST have the correct Fulu fork version.

reachability.notes: Wrong fork version causes complete P2P network isolation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect fork version causes node to operate on incompatible protocol version, effectively forking off the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating wrong fork version after upgrade.

properties.text: After upgrade_to_fulu: post.fork.epoch == get_current_epoch(pre). The fork epoch recorded in state MUST match the epoch at which the fork was triggered.

reachability.notes: Incorrect fork epoch in state would affect epoch-dependent logic.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect fork epoch could affect downstream epoch-dependent computations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing fork epoch mismatch in post-state.

properties.text: After upgrade_to_fulu: post.proposer_lookahead == initialize_proposer_lookahead(pre). The new Fulu field (EIP-7917) MUST be freshly initialized from the pre-state.

reachability.notes: proposer_lookahead initialization affects proposer scheduling.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect proposer_lookahead would cause wrong proposer assignments, enabling unauthorized block proposals.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating incorrect proposer assignment from bad lookahead initialization.

properties.text: upgrade_to_fulu is a pure function: it MUST NOT produce side effects on the pre-state. The pre-state MUST remain unchanged after calling upgrade_to_fulu.

reachability.notes: Pre-state mutation would cause non-deterministic behavior but is hard to exploit externally.

properties.severity_justification: Pre-state mutation during fork would cause non-deterministic state transitions, potentially causing consensus splits.

properties.text: Fork epoch constants MUST be strictly ordered: GENESIS_FORK_EPOCH < ALTAIR_FORK_EPOCH < BELLATRIX_FORK_EPOCH < CAPELLA_FORK_EPOCH < DENEB_FORK_EPOCH < ELECTRA_FORK_EPOCH < FULU_FORK_EPOCH.

reachability.notes: Fork epoch ordering is critical for compute_fork_version correctness. Reachable via P2P fork digest computation.

properties.severity_justification: Violated ordering would cause compute_fork_version to return wrong fork version, causing network-wide P2P partition.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating misordered fork constants causing consensus divergence.

properties.text: compute_fork_version MUST take exactly one branch for any valid epoch value. No two branches may be taken simultaneously, and there MUST be no epoch value for which no branch is taken.

reachability.notes: Used in fork digest computation for P2P topic subscription.

properties.severity_justification: Ambiguous fork version selection would cause P2P network fragmentation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating epoch value that causes wrong or ambiguous fork version.

properties.text: compute_fork_version MUST always return a valid Version (4-byte fork version). The function MUST NOT return an error, null, or undefined value for any epoch >= 0.

reachability.notes: A null fork version would crash P2P message handling.

properties.severity_justification: Null or invalid return would crash node during P2P handshake or gossip validation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with edge-case epoch causing function failure.

properties.text: verify_data_column_sidecar MUST reject any sidecar where sidecar.index >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS. The column index MUST be in range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS).

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft a sidecar with an out-of-range index and send it via P2P gossip.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting out-of-range column index could cause array out-of-bounds access, node crash, or data corruption.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing sidecar with index >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS bypassing validation.

properties.text: verify_data_column_sidecar MUST reject any sidecar where kzg_commitments is empty (len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) == 0).

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with empty commitments and send via gossip.

properties.severity_justification: Empty commitments sidecar could cause division-by-zero or unexpected behavior in downstream processing.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing empty-commitment sidecar bypassing validation.

properties.text: verify_data_column_sidecar MUST reject any sidecar where len(kzg_commitments) > get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block for the epoch derived from the sidecar's slot.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with excessive commitments. The epoch-dependent limit adds complexity.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting excess commitments could cause excessive memory allocation, node crash, or consensus divergence if max_blobs_per_block differs between clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Especially interesting at epoch boundaries where blob parameters change.

properties.text: verify_data_column_sidecar MUST reject any sidecar where len(column) != len(kzg_commitments) or len(column) != len(kzg_proofs). All three arrays MUST have equal length.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with mismatched array lengths.

properties.severity_justification: Mismatched lengths could cause out-of-bounds access during KZG proof verification, crashing the node.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing mismatched-length sidecar bypassing validation.

properties.text: All cells in a column MUST share the same cell index, equal to the column index. verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs MUST construct cell_indices = [CellIndex(sidecar.index)] * len(sidecar.column).

reachability.notes: Incorrect cell index assignment would cause KZG verification to pass for data at wrong positions.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect cell index mapping would break the data availability guarantee, allowing invalid data to pass verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating cell index mismatch causing false KZG proof acceptance.

properties.text: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch MUST receive consistent-length arrays: len(commitments_bytes) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs_bytes).

reachability.notes: Inconsistent array lengths passed to KZG library could cause undefined behavior.

properties.severity_justification: Inconsistent lengths in KZG batch verification could cause crashes or bypass verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing inconsistent array lengths reaching KZG library.

properties.text: KZG_COMMITMENTS_INCLUSION_PROOF_DEPTH MUST equal 4. The Merkle proof linking kzg_commitments to body_root MUST use exactly this depth.

reachability.notes: Incorrect proof depth would cause all inclusion proofs to fail or accept invalid proofs.

properties.severity_justification: Wrong proof depth would cause valid sidecars to be rejected (liveness failure) or invalid sidecars accepted (safety failure).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating proof depth mismatch causing acceptance of forged sidecar.

properties.text: The Merkle inclusion proof MUST correctly link hash_tree_root(sidecar.kzg_commitments) to the BeaconBlockBody root via the blob_kzg_commitments field's generalized index.

reachability.notes: Attacker could craft sidecar with forged inclusion proof.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting sidecar with invalid inclusion proof breaks data availability integrity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing forged inclusion proof bypassing is_valid_merkle_branch.

properties.text: compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar MUST return a value in range [0, DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT) for all valid column_index values. DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT == 128.

reachability.notes: Subnet miscalculation would fragment the gossip network.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect subnet computation causes sidecars to be published to wrong subnets, fragmenting data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating subnet routing error causing data availability failure.

properties.text: In the data_column_sidecar gossip validation pipeline, ALL REJECT checks MUST fail (i.e., return non-REJECT results) before a message is accepted and forwarded to peers.

reachability.notes: An attacker sends crafted sidecars via gossip. Any bypass of a REJECT check is a critical vulnerability.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing any REJECT check allows invalid sidecars to propagate across the network, potentially corrupting data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing a sidecar that bypasses one or more REJECT validation steps.

properties.text: IGNORE conditions in gossip validation MUST allow re-processing of the sidecar later (via queuing). An IGNORED sidecar MUST NOT be permanently discarded or forwarded.

reachability.notes: Incorrect IGNORE handling could cause data loss (permanent discard) or amplification (forwarding).

properties.severity_justification: Permanently discarding IGNORED sidecars degrades data availability. Forwarding them bypasses validation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing IGNORED sidecar being forwarded without re-validation.

properties.text: Exactly one path from q_init to q_final MUST be taken per sidecar during gossip validation. The validation pipeline MUST be deterministic and non-branching (only one outcome per sidecar).

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic validation would cause different nodes to reach different conclusions about the same sidecar.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic validation causes consensus divergence between nodes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating non-deterministic validation outcome for identical sidecar.

properties.text: The deduplication key for data column sidecars MUST be the tuple (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index). Only the first valid sidecar for each unique tuple MUST be accepted; subsequent duplicates MUST be IGNORED.

reachability.notes: An attacker (equivocating proposer) sends conflicting sidecars for the same tuple.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting conflicting sidecars for the same tuple allows equivocation, breaking data availability consistency.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing duplicate or conflicting sidecar acceptance for same dedup key.

properties.text: max_blobs_per_block MUST be epoch-dependent via get_blob_parameters. The beacon block KZG validation MUST use the epoch derived from the current state to look up the correct limit.

reachability.notes: A proposer could include excess blob commitments hoping the validator uses the wrong epoch's limit.

properties.severity_justification: Using wrong epoch for blob parameter lookup could accept blocks with excess blobs, causing consensus divergence between clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Especially critical at epoch boundaries where blob parameters change.

properties.text: Blocks with zero blob KZG commitments MUST be considered valid. There is no lower bound check on the number of commitments.

reachability.notes: Rejecting zero-commitment blocks would be a liveness issue.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrectly rejecting zero-commitment blocks causes liveness failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing valid empty-blob block being rejected.

properties.text: DataColumnSidecarsByRange responses MUST contain no more than count * NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS sidecars AND no more than MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS sidecars total.

reachability.notes: Attacker sends large range request to exhaust responding node's resources.

properties.severity_justification: Unbounded response size causes memory exhaustion and node crash (DoS).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing response exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS.

properties.text: DataColumnSidecarsByRange sidecars MUST be sent in (slot, column_index) ordering. Responses MUST be ordered deterministically.

reachability.notes: Misordered responses could confuse requesting nodes.

properties.severity_justification: Misordered responses could cause incorrect data reconstruction at the requesting node.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating misordered response causing data corruption.

properties.text: For DataColumnSidecarsByRange, if any sidecar from a block is included in the response, ALL sidecars from that block MUST be included.

reachability.notes: Partial block sidecars could cause incomplete data availability assessment.

properties.severity_justification: Partial sidecar sets could cause data availability sampling failures.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating partial block sidecars causing availability failure.

properties.text: DataColumnSidecarsByRange sidecars MUST be from the current fork choice chain. If fork choice changes during response, the stream MUST terminate.

reachability.notes: Serving sidecars from a non-canonical chain could mislead requesting nodes.

properties.severity_justification: Serving data from non-canonical chain could cause requesting node to accept invalid chain.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating non-canonical data served during fork choice change.

properties.text: data_column_serve_range MUST equal [max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), current_epoch]. Requests outside this range MUST return ResourceUnavailable(3).

reachability.notes: Attacker requests data outside serve range to force historical data access.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect serve range could leak historical data or cause denial of valid requests.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing serve range bypass.

properties.text: DataColumnSidecarsByRoot MUST reject requests where requested_columns_count > MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS.

reachability.notes: Attacker sends oversized request to exhaust node resources.

properties.severity_justification: Unbounded requests cause resource exhaustion (DoS).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing request exceeding limit being processed.

properties.text: DataColumnSidecarsByRoot MUST only serve sidecars for blocks that pass gossip validation. Sidecars MUST NOT be served for blocks failing beacon-chain state transition.

reachability.notes: Serving invalid-block sidecars could mislead requesting peers.

properties.severity_justification: Serving sidecars for invalid blocks could propagate invalid data.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating invalid-block sidecar being served via RPC.

properties.text: Blobs retrieved from the local execution layer via engine_getBlobsV2 MUST skip cryptographic verification (they are trusted by construction). However, if not ALL blobs matching kzg_commitments are retrieved, the distributed publishing process MUST abort.

reachability.notes: Compromised EL could inject invalid blob data. This is an intentional trust assumption but a critical one.

properties.severity_justification: Compromised EL injecting invalid blobs causes invalid data columns to propagate across network, undermining data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating EL blob injection causing network-wide invalid data propagation.

properties.text: Data column sidecar publishing MUST only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (via custody or sampling duties). Non-subscribed subnets MUST NOT receive published sidecars from this node.

reachability.notes: Publishing to non-subscribed subnets is unauthorized.

properties.severity_justification: Unauthorized subnet publishing could influence data availability beyond designated role.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating unauthorized subnet publishing.

properties.text: The anti-equivocation cache MUST be updated regardless of whether data column sidecar publishing occurs. Even if the node is not subscribed to the subnet, the cache MUST be updated.

reachability.notes: Failure to update cache could allow later acceptance of conflicting sidecars.

properties.severity_justification: Missing cache update allows equivocation: accepting conflicting sidecars from same proposer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing equivocation due to missing cache update.

properties.text: Imported data columns from distributed blob publishing MUST behave as if received via gossip. They MUST follow the same validation and forwarding rules.

reachability.notes: Bypassing gossip validation for locally-generated columns could create inconsistency.

properties.severity_justification: Inconsistent treatment of imported vs. gossip columns could cause subtle consensus issues.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating divergent behavior for imported vs gossip columns.

properties.text: BlobSidecarsByRange is deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS. After full deprecation, the endpoint MUST respond with empty list or rejection.

reachability.notes: Peers may request deprecated blob sidecars to exploit transition period.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect handling of deprecated endpoint could cause resource waste but limited security impact.

properties.text: During the Fulu transition period, BlobSidecarsByRange MUST serve only pre-Fulu blobs within the range [min(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), FULU_FORK_EPOCH). Requests entirely at or after FULU_FORK_EPOCH MUST receive empty responses.

reachability.notes: Incorrect range computation could serve Fulu-era data via deprecated endpoint.

properties.severity_justification: Serving wrong-era data via deprecated API could confuse syncing nodes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing Fulu-era data served via deprecated endpoint.

properties.text: Peers MUST NOT be penalized for requesting blob sidecars from FULU_FORK_EPOCH during the transition period. This applies to both BlobSidecarsByRange and BlobSidecarsByRoot.

reachability.notes: Penalizing transition-period requests could partition syncing nodes from the network.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect penalization could cause legitimate syncing nodes to be disconnected from the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating peer penalization for legitimate transition-period requests.

properties.text: ENR eth2 value MUST always be exactly 16 bytes (SSZ-encoded ENRForkID). For BPO forks, next_fork_version MUST equal current_fork_version while next_fork_epoch MUST be set to the planned epoch.

reachability.notes: Incorrect ENR encoding causes peering failures, especially during BPO fork transitions.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect ENR encoding causes peer discovery failures, potentially isolating nodes from the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing ENR encoding failure during BPO fork transition.

properties.text: When no fork is planned, ENR MUST set next_fork_version = current_fork_version and next_fork_epoch = FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH.

reachability.notes: Incorrect no-fork ENR values could cause premature peering disconnects.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect ENR values when no fork is planned could cause unnecessary peer disconnections.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating peering failure from incorrect no-fork ENR.

properties.text: ENR epoch MUST be determined by wall-clock time, not sync state. A syncing node MUST advertise the correct current epoch in its ENR regardless of how far behind it is in sync.

reachability.notes: Sync-state-based epoch would cause syncing nodes to advertise stale fork digests.

properties.severity_justification: Stale epoch in ENR would cause peer discovery to fail for syncing nodes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating sync-state-based ENR causing peering failure.

properties.text: A malicious peer MUST NOT be able to forge a data column sidecar that passes the BLS proposer signature check (q_check_proposer_sig) without possessing the legitimate proposer's private key.

reachability.notes: Attacker sends forged sidecar with fabricated proposer signature via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Forged proposer identity would allow injecting arbitrary data columns into the network, breaking data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing forged BLS signature passing verification (would imply break in BLS scheme or implementation bug).

properties.text: Three-layer integrity verification MUST hold for all accepted sidecars: (1) structural validity via verify_data_column_sidecar, (2) cryptographic cell-commitment binding via verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs, (3) Merkle inclusion proof via verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof.

reachability.notes: Attacker attempts to inject tampered data columns via gossip.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing any integrity layer allows injection of invalid data, breaking data availability guarantees.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing tampered sidecar bypassing any of the three verification layers.

properties.text: Gossip validation checks MUST be ordered from cheapest to most expensive to minimize resource waste from invalid messages. Structural checks first, then subnet validation, then slot timing, then signature verification, then parent/ancestry checks, and KZG/Merkle proofs last.

reachability.notes: Attacker floods gossip with invalid sidecars to waste CPU on expensive checks.

properties.severity_justification: Performing expensive KZG verification before cheap structural checks amplifies DoS impact.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating misordered checks causing amplified DoS.

properties.text: A non-proposer node MUST NOT be able to get a sidecar accepted by setting proposer_index to a scheduled proposer. The validation pipeline MUST verify proposer_index matches the expected proposer for the slot AFTER signature verification.

reachability.notes: Attacker attempts privilege escalation by claiming to be a scheduled proposer.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting unauthorized proposer would allow injecting arbitrary data into the data availability layer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing non-proposer sidecar accepted.

properties.text: The p256verify precompile MUST NOT revert under any circumstances except out-of-gas. Invalid inputs MUST return empty bytes (0 bytes) rather than causing a revert.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits transaction invoking p256verify with crafted inputs via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Unexpected revert could cause consensus split between clients that do and do not revert.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing input that causes revert in specific client implementation.

properties.text: p256verify input validation MUST be performed in the exact sequential order: (1) length check (160 bytes), (2) r bounds [1, n-1], (3) s bounds [1, n-1], (4) qx bounds [0, p-1], (5) qy bounds [0, p-1], (6) curve equation check, (7) infinity check. All clients MUST follow this exact order.

reachability.notes: Different validation orderings between clients cause consensus splits for edge-case inputs.

properties.severity_justification: Validation order divergence between clients causes consensus split for edge-case inputs that fail at different steps.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing input that produces different results with different validation orderings.

properties.text: The point-at-infinity check on R' during p256verify signature verification is MANDATORY. Omitting this check leads to implementation-dependent non-deterministic behavior.

reachability.notes: Missing infinity check is a known RIP-7212 vulnerability fix.

properties.severity_justification: Missing infinity check allows forged signatures to pass verification, enabling unauthorized actions in P256-secured contracts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing forged signature passing verification due to missing infinity check.

properties.text: p256verify signature comparison MUST use modular reduction (r' == r mod n) rather than direct equality. Direct comparison fails for edge-case x-coordinates exceeding the subgroup order.

reachability.notes: Known RIP-7212 vulnerability fix. Direct comparison causes consensus splits.

properties.severity_justification: Non-modular comparison causes consensus split for signatures involving x-coordinates near or exceeding subgroup order n.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing consensus divergence from direct vs modular comparison.

properties.text: The gas cap GAS_CAP = 16777216 (2^24) MUST be enforced at exactly two layers: (1) transaction pool admission, and (2) block validation. Any transaction with gasLimit > 16777216 MUST be rejected at both layers.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits transaction with gasLimit > 2^24 via P2P or private channels.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing gas cap allows single transaction to consume excessive block resources.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing gas cap bypass at either txpool or block validation layer.

properties.text: Block validation MUST iterate over ALL transactions and reject the entire block if ANY single transaction has gasLimit > 16777216. There MUST be no early-exit optimization that skips checking some transactions.

reachability.notes: Malicious builder places oversized transaction at end of block to exploit early-exit optimizations.

properties.severity_justification: Skipping gas cap check for any transaction allows oversized transactions into blocks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing transaction at specific position bypassing gas cap check.

properties.text: The gas cap value (16777216) MUST be identical at both the txpool validation layer and the block validation layer. Divergence MUST NOT occur between these two enforcement points.

reachability.notes: Divergent constants could be caused by implementation bug in one layer.

properties.severity_justification: Different gas cap values between layers would cause blocks to be built but rejected, or vice versa.

properties.text: The gas cap MUST apply uniformly to ALL transaction types (legacy, EIP-2930, EIP-1559, EIP-4844 blob transactions) regardless of the block gas limit set by validators.

reachability.notes: Attacker could use specific transaction type to bypass gas cap check.

properties.severity_justification: Transaction-type-specific bypass of gas cap allows oversized transactions via specific types.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing specific transaction type bypassing gas cap.

properties.text: The ModExp gas cost formula (EIP-7883) MUST be deterministic: for identical inputs (base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length, data), ALL client implementations MUST compute the identical gas cost value.

reachability.notes: Different gas computation between clients causes consensus split.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic gas computation causes consensus split as nodes disagree on transaction validity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing input producing different gas costs in different clients.

properties.text: multiplication_complexity MUST have a minimum output of 16 for all inputs. For inputs with max(base_length, modulus_length) <= 32, the multiplication_complexity MUST be fixed at 16.

reachability.notes: Attacker crafts ModExp inputs to minimize gas cost relative to computation.

properties.severity_justification: Zero or near-zero complexity allows cheap execution of expensive operations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing inputs producing complexity < 16.

properties.text: The minimum gas floor for ModExp MUST be 500. The final gas cost MUST be max(500, floor(multiplication_complexity * iteration_count)). The division by 3 from EIP-2565 MUST NOT be present.

reachability.notes: Residual /3 divisor from EIP-2565 would undercharge by 3x.

properties.severity_justification: Gas floor too low or residual /3 divisor allows cheap ModExp execution, enabling resource exhaustion DoS.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing ModExp call charged less than 500 gas or with /3 divisor.

properties.text: Client implementations MUST use sufficiently large integer types (256-bit or arbitrary precision) for ModExp gas calculations. The computation 2 * words^2 and complexity * iterations MUST NOT overflow for any valid input.

reachability.notes: Integer overflow in gas calculation wraps to small value, undercharging.

properties.severity_justification: Integer overflow in gas calculation allows near-free execution of expensive ModExp operations, enabling severe DoS.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing integer overflow in gas calculation producing near-zero gas cost.

properties.text: parse_deposit_requests MUST perform a two-layer filter for deposit events: (1) log.address == DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS, AND (2) log.topics[0] == DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH. Only logs matching both conditions MUST be processed.

reachability.notes: Attacker deploys contract emitting fake deposit events to inject fraudulent deposits.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing deposit contract filter allows injecting fraudulent deposits into consensus layer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing fraudulent deposit event accepted from non-deposit-contract address.

properties.text: extract_deposit_data MUST validate: total data length == 576 bytes, PUBKEY_OFFSET == 160, WC_OFFSET == 256, AMOUNT_OFFSET == 320, SIGNATURE_OFFSET == 384, INDEX_OFFSET == 512. Any mismatch MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Malformed deposit event data could cause incorrect deposit extraction or crash.

properties.severity_justification: Malformed deposit data bypassing validation could inject corrupted deposits or crash the execution client.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing malformed deposit data bypassing offset/size validation.

properties.text: compute_requests_hash MUST use a two-level SHA-256 construction: hash each individual request, then combine all digests in a final outer hash. The order of requests MUST be deterministic and identical across all clients.

reachability.notes: Different request hash between clients causes consensus split.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect requests_hash causes consensus divergence between execution clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing different clients producing different requests_hash for same block.

properties.text: EIP-7702 Authorization MUST validate: (1) chain_id matches current chain, (2) nonce matches authorizer's current nonce, (3) ECDSA signature (y_parity, r, s) recovers to the expected authorizer address. All three checks MUST pass before authorization is accepted.

reachability.notes: Attacker forges Authorization to steal victim's account via unauthorized code delegation.

properties.severity_justification: Forged authorization allows taking control of victim's account, enabling fund theft.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing forged or replayed authorization bypassing validation.

properties.text: encode_account MUST produce deterministic and canonical RLP encoding with tuple ordering (nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash). The storage_root MUST be a valid 32-byte Merkle root (though validation is the caller's responsibility).

reachability.notes: Non-canonical RLP encoding would cause state root divergence between clients.

properties.severity_justification: Non-canonical encoding causes state root divergence, splitting the network.
